methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Retro Biosciences is a Series A-stage biotech company seeking valuation within a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public comparable companies in the Biotech & Health sector. The target has raised $180M to date.

Two valuation methods prove applicable. The Stage-Progression method anchors to the $180M raised as a $1.0B proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implying a $2.0B valuation. The Peer-Median method examines six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—including Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yielding a median of $805M with a range of $100M to $3.3B. Public-Comp Anchoring is inapplicable due to the absence of disclosed revenue data for the private target.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a valuation range of $684M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.4B. This 3.4x spread reflects material uncertainty around the company's technology maturity, capital requirements to clinical inflection, and competitive positioning relative to the peer cohort. The peer-median method suggests conservatism at the lower bound; the stage-progression method supports the upper bound. Resolution of valuation risk depends on near-term evidence of scientific or clinical progress and trajectory toward Series B fundraising metrics observed in the comparable set.
